title: Port number field is not used / ignored on web connections
author: skewty
body_snippet:
## Expected Behavior
When protocol is set to HTTP or HTTPS 
Hostname/IP field must not contain port number of server.
because port number is specified in Port field.

## Current Behavior
Port number specified in Port field is ignored. The port number must be embedded in Hostname/IP field.

## Possible Solution
- Remove `Port` number field and `Protocol` fields for Browser connections as the URI contains all the required information.

Version 1.77.2

iterations.description: The current URL construction logic in HTTPBase.cs can result in double port numbers if a port is embedded in the hostname field and also specified in the port field.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The current URL construction logic in HTTPBase.cs can result in double port numbers if a port is embedded in the hostname field and also specified in the port field.
Approach: Refactor the GetUrl() method in HTTPBase.cs to use UriBuilder for robust URL construction, correctly handling embedded ports in the hostname and prioritizing explicit port field values while avoiding double-port issues.
